$comment: Meta-schema for a component-family parameter dictionary. A dictionary defines the canonical parameter keys, units, groups, and vendor aliases for one family such as ldo, buck_converter, or mosfet. The datasheet schema stays family-agnostic. The dictionary supplies the vocabulary.
title: Family Parameter Dictionary

unit.description: The canonical base-SI unit for this parameter. A conformance runner SHOULD reject a measurement whose unit is neither this nor one of altUnits, so an off-dimension unit (temperature in volts) is caught.

altUnits.description: Additional units a measurement of this parameter may legitimately use, for parameters that vendors specify more than one way (line regulation as ppm/V or V/V; drift/hysteresis as ppm, %, or V; accuracy as % or absolute V). Every entry MUST be in the schema's unit enum. Omit for single-unit parameters so their dimension is strictly enforced.

array.description: True when typically specified across multiple conditions (per load current, per frequency).

conditionAxis.description: The condition axis that distinguishes array entries, e.g. "I_OUT" for dropout, "F" for PSRR.

iecCdd.description: IEC 61360 Common Data Dictionary concept, where a clean mapping exists.

optional.description: True for feature-gated params (power-good, sink current) present only on some parts.
